working_on: Routine task P5-12: Phase 5 closeout (engine/build_readiness/phase_5_closeout.md + ENGINE_LOG + STATE.md + optional close ADR) — sixteenth and final routine-mode session against T-PHASE-5; closes the last of 16 explicit Phase 5 tasks per ADR 0052 decomposition. declared_scope: Author engine/build_readiness/phase_5_closeout.md as a first-of-its-kind closeout report for the project. Shape derives from phase_5.md 'Success criteria' section (lines 298-306). Sections: (1) Per-subdomain coverage — table of node/edge counts per subdomain extracted from migration contract headers (totals: 380 nodes / 536 edges); per-subdomain confidence_level composition observations where derivable; granularity-principle observations on subdomains that produced denser/sparser concept sets than expected; (2) Soft-warn telemetry trends across S-0050 → S-0075 — per-category trend table; the dominant signal is missing_rigor_score growing monotonically from 25 (S-0050) to 366 (S-0075) per the choice not to backfill rigor scores at seed time (carries forward to Phase 6 self-correction); secondary persistent: issue_collision (consistent low background); health_check_overdue (fired late at S-0063 and S-0075); zero hard-fails across all 16 routine sessions; (3) Predicate registry final state — record the S-0075 T1-E/T3-B adjudication (cross_domain_dependency NOT promoted, reserved-but-unused row removed in same commit); active registry remains the v1 two-predicate set (pedagogical_prerequisite covering both within-domain and cross-domain; historical_influence reserved-but-unused awaiting Phase 6); (4) Tier 1/2/3 forward-pointer disposition — walk through phase_5.md's 7 T1 + 8 T2 + 7 T3 entries; record which were honored as authored, which evolved during execution; (5) Per-task closure summary — the 16 tasks plus the resumption pattern at S-0050 (P5-01a closed_partial → completed at S-0053 boot rollover) and S-0063 (P5-06 closed_partial → completed at S-0068); (6) Mechanism exercise summary — apply_migration.py wrapper (S-0066, S-0068, S-0070, S-0071, S-0073, S-0074, S-0075 — seven exercises including S-0073's exit-3 SQL-error rollback path); routine_lifecycle_push.py wrapper (S-0061+); routine_boot_freshness.py (S-0072+); (7) Cadence-overdue audit-defer — record that S-0076 boot surfaced health_check_overdue (slots_since=11 vs cadence=10; overdue by 1; last_audit=S-0065); STATE.md framed S-0076 as 'natural surface for the project health-check audit' because closeout is itself audit-shaped; the audit-shaped *content* relevant to Phase 5 (per-subdomain coverage trends, soft-warn telemetry trends, predicate registry final state) is folded into this closeout deliverable; the *formal* project-wide health audit acceptance (author docs/health-checks/S-NNNN.md, run scan_orphans.py, bump last_audit_session) requires the discretionary 'Run audit now or defer' judgment and is properly an interactive /start-engine action; routine-mode cannot adjudicate; trigger continues firing until the next interactive session lands the formal audit. No supersession ADR — Phase 5 surfaced no finding warranting ADR 0052 supersession (decomposition executed cleanly, epistemology-first calibration held, pre-split/single-task discipline produced session-sized work, cross-bridges-runs-last contained cross-domain edge collisions, service nodes pass produced clean primitives, out-of-scope deferrals — philosophy of religion, history-as-subdomain — remain admissible per the class-boundary clause but were not pressed by Phase 5 execution). One small Issue filing planned: 'engine/build_readiness/README.md should index closeout-class reports separately from gate-class reports' (build_readiness/README.md is NOT in P5-12 scope_lock, so the README index update is properly deferred). End-state: P5-12 marked complete in auto_target.json; check_target.py shows all 16 tasks PASS; T-PHASE-5 fully closed; auto_target.json target-met; phase: P_5
